intra_handlers.py::if not section:::#1.note: _route_entry_runway only runs on an entry-port -> internal-station edge; the target of such an edge always carries a section_id that resolves, so the guard's return-None arm protects against a malformed graph rather than a topology.

intra_handlers.py::if section.direction.note: Auto-layout only ever assigns a LEFT (flow-side) entry to an LR section (0/225 LR entries are non-LEFT across the corpus); a RIGHT/back-side entry on an LR section is a misconfiguration, so the non-flow-side reject arm guards rather than encodes a valid topology.

Mirror of the LR guard: RL sections only ever receive RIGHT (flow-side) entries (all 24 corpus RL entries are RIGHT); a LEFT-side entry on an RL section is a misconfiguration the reject arm guards against.

No TB/BT entry reaches _route_entry_runway: TB LEFT/RIGHT entries are consumed by _route_tb_lr_entry and TB/BT TOP/BOTTOM entries by _route_perp_entry (both earlier in the dispatch chain), and a BT section is never produced (the direction directive accepts only LR/RL/TB; 0/391 corpus sections are BT). Left in place as a cheap backstop pending the deletion review in #689.

intra_handlers.py::if not st or st.is_po.note: The loop already skips edge.target and every id in section.port_ids, so a surviving id resolves to a non-port internal station; the not-st / is_port re-check is belt-and-suspenders against a port absent from port_ids.

intra_handlers.py::if dx <= 0 and abs(dy.note: The not-same_section precondition is never satisfied at _route_intra_section: cross-section edges are consumed by _route_inter_section upstream (0/391 corpus fixtures reach this branch with differing sections), and a sectionless flat graph's serpentine fold is routed before this handler too (verified: a flat fold over the threshold does not reach the arm). The cross-row fold branch is shadowed. Left in place pending the deletion review in #689.

intra_handlers.py::if not (same_sec or i.note: _is_side_branch_ascent reaches this classification (44/391 corpus fixtures) but no fixture takes the reject arm: a target sitting within offset_step*2 of the source section's trunk Y is always a same-section internal station or that section's exit port. The arm needs a target at the source's trunk Y that belongs to a different section or is a non-exit port, which the trunk-alignment geometry does not produce. Left in place pending the deletion review in #689.

intra_handlers.py::if room < src_min + c.note: The entry-runway 'too tight' return cannot fire: room = |first_x - entry_port_x| is floored at SECTION_X_PADDING (50px, the entry port sits at section.bbox_x = min(internal_station_x) - SECTION_X_PADDING), while the threshold src_min + diagonal_run = CURVE_RADIUS(10) + MIN_STRAIGHT_PORT(5) + DIAGONAL_RUN(30) = 45px. Since 50 >= 45 the guard is always False; SECTION_X_PADDING is not exposed as a directive. Left in place pending the deletion review in #689.

intra_handlers.py::if abs(dx) < COORD_TO.note: A same-section, non-TB intra edge with |dx| < COORD_TOLERANCE (1px) and non-zero dy is not constructible: layering assigns increasing X along edges so the minimum |dx| at _route_intra_section across the corpus is 50px (port spacing / off-track output lead). TB same-track edges that could reach |dx|=0 are intercepted by _route_tb_internal before this handler. Left in place pending the deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if s.bbox_h <= 0:::#1.note: Degenerate-bbox guard in _build_row_bands: a laid-out section always has bbox_h > 0, so the skip arm only protects against a zero/negative-height section that the layout never produces.

normalize.py::if s.bbox_w <= 0:::#1.note: Degenerate-bbox guard in _section_intrudes: a laid-out section always has bbox_w > 0; the skip arm guards a zero/negative-width section the layout never produces.

normalize.py::if s.bbox_w <= 0:::#2.note: Degenerate-bbox guard in _h_segment_crosses_other_section: mirror of the bbox_w<=0 guard; sections always have positive width.

normalize.py::if vi < 0 or vi + 1 >= len.note: Bounds guard in _peeloff_riser: the trunk index comes from range(1, len(pts)-2), keeping vi and vi+1 in range; the out-of-bounds return arm is unreachable for a valid trunk.

normalize.py::if abs(x1 - x0) > COORD_TO.note: Re-validation guard in _peeloff_riser: the leg flanking a trunk is already proven vertical by _collect_htrunks (L392/L394), so the not-vertical return arm cannot fire.

normalize.py::if len(per_line) < 2:::#1.note: Caller-guarded in _respace_risers_to_trunk: the caller only invokes it when the cluster holds >=2 distinct line_ids (L515), so per_line always has >=2 entries; the early-return arm is unreachable.

normalize.py::if rp.curve_radii is None:.note: curve_radii is always a concrete list for inter-section routes (every inter-section handler sets it); the None arm in _set_vchannel_x is a belt-and-suspenders guard.

normalize.py::if 0 <= radius_idx < len(r.note: Bounds guard in _set_vchannel_x: radius_idx in (k-1, k) for a real channel stays in range; the out-of-range skip arm is defensive.

normalize.py::if abs(x2 - x1) > COORD_TO.note: Reject arm in _initial_fanout_descent: a route whose first segment is horizontal (passes the prior check) always has a vertical second segment in the orthogonal inter-section route model; the non-vertical reject mirrors the proven-unreachable _final_port_approach reject (L687) and never fires.

normalize.py::elif far_y < t.y - COORD_T.note: Degenerate-leg guard in _trunk_slot_features: a trunk's flanking legs are genuine vertical legs (far_y differs from the trunk y), so neither the below nor the above classification is skipped; the fall-through arm needs a zero-height leg the layout never produces.

normalize.py::if feats is None:::#1.note: Unused default-arg branch: all three callers of _band_order_crossings pass feats explicitly, so the feats-is-None compute-it arm is never taken.

normalize.py::if top is None or bottom i.note: Defensive in _inter_row_gap_band: adjacent rows in the sorted section-row set both contain sections, so row_bottom_edge / row_top_edge resolve; the None skip arm guards a row with no sections that the iteration never yields.

normalize.py::if rp.curve_radii is None:.note: curve_radii is always set for inter-section routes; the None arm in _restack_htrunk is a defensive guard.

normalize.py::if 0 <= k - 1 < len(rp.cur.note: Bounds guard in _restack_htrunk: trunk index k >= 1 so k-1 >= 0 is always in range; the skip arm is defensive.

normalize.py::if k < len(rp.curve_radii).note: Bounds guard in _restack_htrunk: for a real interior trunk k and k+2 are in range; the skip arm is defensive.

normalize.py::if rp.curve_radii is None:.note: curve_radii is always set for inter-section routes; the None arm in _restack_channel is a defensive guard.

normalize.py::if 0 <= k - 1 < len(rp.cur.note: Bounds guard in _restack_channel: the skip arm fires only for k==0 (channel at the route start), which the restacked gap channels (interior verticals after a horizontal lead-in) never are.

normalize.py::if k < len(rp.curve_radii).note: Bounds guard in _restack_channel: the skip arm guards k or k+2 out of range for a real interior channel; defensive.

normalize.py::if not (left + COORD_TOLER.note: Float-precision guard in _bucket_gap_channels: routing centres channels in inter-column gaps with >= 16px clearance from section edges (min observed across the corpus), far outside the 1px COORD_TOLERANCE edge band, so the on/outside-edge arm is never entered.

normalize.py::if not (left <= ch.x <= ri.note: Downstream of the gap-edge band guard (L243); only reached when a channel x sits within 1px of a gap boundary, which the >=16px clearance never produces, so both arms are unreachable.

normalize.py::if top + total > limit:::#.note: No-clamp return arm in _clamp_inter_row_band_top: the inter-row gap is auto-widened to fit its routing bundle, so a fanned trunk band sits at or over the header-clearance limit and the clamp arm is taken; the load-bearing no-clamp return is structurally avoided by the gap sizing.

normalize.py::if band is not None:::#1.note: Same-line dogleg: the band-found arm (->1215) is reachable only via the same-line regime whose down-move crowds the next-row header (#698); the band-is-None arm (->1219) is defensive (both colliding trunks route through _center_inter_row_channel, so the trunk Y always resolves to a gap band). Exercise ->1215 with a clean fixture once #698 is fixed.

normalize.py::if prefer_down and down >=.note: Same-line dogleg: the down-move arm (->1225) is reachable only via the same-line regime whose down-move crowds the next-row header (#698); the elif/else arm (->1226) is defensive (the two fused flows start at an identical Y so prefer_down is always True and down >= min_sep holds). Exercise ->1225 with a clean fixture once #698 is fixed.

normalize.py::elif up >= min_sep:::#1.note: Same-line dogleg move-up branch: unreachable because prefer_down is always True (the two flows start at the same Y), so the down branch is always taken before this elif.

normalize.py::if band is not None:::#2.note: Distinct-line dogleg: the band-found arm (->1252) is reachable only via the distinct dogleg whose routing crosses the exempt trunk twice (#702); the band-is-None arm (->1256) is defensive (the trunk Y always resolves to an inter-row gap band). Exercise ->1252 with a clean fixture once #702 renders cleanly.

normalize.py::if (t.y >= hit.y and below.note: Distinct-line dogleg: the below-move arm (->1258) is reachable only via the distinct dogleg that crosses the exempt trunk twice (#702); the move-up arm (->1259) is defensive (the trunk starts at the obstacle Y inside a valid band, so the below branch is always taken). Exercise ->1258 with a clean fixture once #702 renders cleanly.

normalize.py::elif above_ok:::#1.note: Distinct-line dogleg move-up/skip branch: unreachable because the below branch is always taken (t.y == hit.y, below_ok true); covers both the move-up and the gap-too-tight continue.

normalize.py::if len(trunks) < 2:::#2.note: _suboptimal_trunk_bands is a validate=True diagnostic (raised by guards.py:_guard_*), not a production render-path gate, so the rendering coverage sweep never reaches it -- analogous to the excluded invariants.py. Exercised by the validator suite, not by fixtures.

normalize.py::for grp in groups:::#3.note: _suboptimal_trunk_bands diagnostic loop: validate-only, not reached by the render-path coverage sweep (see the len(trunks)<2 note).

normalize.py::if len({id(t.route) for t.note: _suboptimal_trunk_bands diagnostic guard: validate-only, not on the render path.

normalize.py::if not any(not t.route.nor.note: _suboptimal_trunk_bands diagnostic guard: validate-only, not on the render path.

normalize.py::for sign in (1, -1):::#1.note: _suboptimal_trunk_bands direction loop: validate-only, not on the render path.

normalize.py::if len(slots) < 2 or len(s.note: _suboptimal_trunk_bands slot-count guard: validate-only, not on the render path.

normalize.py::if best < cur:::#1.note: _suboptimal_trunk_bands crossing-comparison: validate-only, not on the render path.

normalize.py::if left - COORD_TOLERANCE.note: Row-agnostic union-gap fallback in _match_channel_gap is provably unreachable: the union gap [max(col_lo right edges), min(col_hi left edges)] is a subset of every per-row gap that brackets a channel, so overlap_match/bracket_match always resolve first and the union fallback is never reached. Left in place pending the deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if abs(pts[k - 1][0] - x0).note: Interior horizontal segments only occur in 6-point bypass U-shapes whose flanking legs are always vertical (same x on each side); routes with non-vertical flanks are normalize_exempt and filtered out. The prev-flank-non-vertical reject in _collect_htrunks is unreachable. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if abs(pts[k + 2][0] - x1).note: Mirror of the prev-flank guard: a bypass trunk's trailing flank is always vertical, so the next-flank-non-vertical reject in _collect_htrunks is unreachable. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if abs(x3 - x2) > COORD_TO.note: Every non-exempt inter-section route ends with a vertical descent into the target Y then a short horizontal port lead (V->H tail); routes with non-V tails are normalize_exempt. The second-to-last-not-vertical reject in _final_port_approach is unreachable. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if sg[0].sign_x != t.sign_.note: Both callers of _coincident_trunk_slots pre-filter input to a single traversal direction (band = [t for t in grp if t.sign_x == sign]), so within the function sg[0].sign_x != t.sign_x is always False; the opposing-direction continue is unreachable. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if down is None or len(up..note: Every line entering a fan-out junction also exits it to an entry port, so _fanout_route_maps always pairs a downstream route for each (jid, line_id) upstream; all handlers emit >=2-point routes. The skip arm in _join_fanout_upstream_tails is unreachable. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if rep_x[a] != rep_x[b]:::.note: The routing system assigns distinct x offsets to every line sharing a column gap, so two distinct lines in one gap-bundle corridor never share a representative x; the equal-rep_x tiebreak fall-through in _distinct_line_order is unreachable (exhaustive corpus scan confirms). Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if abs(ly - pts[1][1]) < C.note: A re-stacked gap channel at points-index 1 always has a horizontal lead-in (L-shape and bypass routes open with a horizontal segment); routes opening non-horizontally are normalize_exempt and excluded from _collect_vchannels. The non-horizontal-lead return in _restack_channel is unreachable. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::for _ in range(8):::#1.note: The 8-iteration exhaustion arm of _clear_channel_x_in_band requires 9+ sections chained across a single column band to shift the channel x eight times without resolving, which no constructible topology produces. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::elif right_ok:::#1.note: The both-bounds-fail return of _clear_channel_x_in_band requires a blocking section spanning the entire inter-column gap (so neither cleared edge fits within bound_left/bound_right); a cross-row section cannot span a gap defined as the space between same-row sections. Unreachable. Pending deletion review in #689.

normalize.py::if rc is not None:::#1.note: Reachable riser-collection arm in _align_peeloff_riser_gaps (a trunk in the group that does not peel off on the tested side). It lives in the peel-off riser respace pass whose body inverts bundle order (#695); a clean fixture exercising it awaits that fix.

normalize.py::if len(risers) < 2:::#1.note: Reachable riser-collection arm (a peel-off side that collects fewer than two risers). In the peel-off riser respace pass under review in #695; exercise once the bundle-order inversion is fixed and a clean peel-off fixture renders.

normalize.py::if not (0 <= lead_i < len(.note: Reachable peel-off edge arm in _peeloff_riser (a trunk at the route start/end with no lead segment). In the peel-off pass under review in #695; deferred with that fix.

normalize.py::if abs(pts[lead_i][1] - pt.note: Reachable peel-off edge arm in _peeloff_riser (a riser whose lead segment is not horizontal). In the peel-off pass under review in #695; deferred with that fix.

normalize.py::if abs(lx - pts[ly_idx][0].note: Reachable peel-off edge arm in _peeloff_riser (a riser whose lead has zero horizontal extent). In the peel-off pass under review in #695; deferred with that fix.

normalize.py::if all(abs(target_x[lid] -.note: Body of _respace_risers_to_trunk (the spacing-disagrees path that performs the respace). Reachable only via the peel-off topology that exposes the bundle-order inversion in #695; a fixture awaits that fix.

normalize.py::for _ty, rc in risers:::#1.note: Respace loop in _respace_risers_to_trunk, reached only when the riser spacing disagrees with the trunk spacing -- the case that triggers the bundle-order inversion in #695. Deferred with that fix.

normalize.py::if rp.curve_radii is None.note: Radii-sizing guard in _set_riser_x_and_radii, reached only when the defective respace pass runs (#695). The curve_radii-is-None sub-arm is additionally defensive (always set for inter-section routes). Deferred with the #695 fix.

normalize.py::for corner_idx, radius_idx.note: Corner loop in _set_riser_x_and_radii, reached only when the defective respace pass runs (#695). Deferred with that fix.

normalize.py::if not (0 <= nbr_idx < len.note: Neighbour-index guard in _set_riser_x_and_radii, reached only when the defective respace pass runs (#695). The out-of-bounds sub-arm is additionally defensive. Deferred with the #695 fix.

normalize.py::if is_lead:::#1.note: Lead/trail corner branch in _set_riser_x_and_radii, reached only when the defective respace pass runs (#695). Deferred with that fix.

normalize.py::if not (0 <= radius_idx <.note: Radius-index guard in _set_riser_x_and_radii, reached only when the defective respace pass runs (#695). The out-of-bounds sub-arm is additionally defensive. Deferred with the #695 fix.

normalize.py::if not is_lead and not (k.note: Trail-corner end-of-route guard in _set_riser_x_and_radii, reached only when the defective respace pass runs (#695). The out-of-bounds sub-arm is additionally defensive. Deferred with the #695 fix.

normalize.py::if lo <= s.grid_col <= hi:.note: The out-of-range-section skip arm of _has_other_row_section_in_col_range is reachable, but the only minimal merge topology found that reaches it triggers the merge-trunk route-through-section defect in #696 (a merge source sitting right of the target). Minimal forward-edge merge fixtures either miss the predicate or trip _guard_routes_enter_sections_at_ports. Exercise via a clean fixture once #696 is resolved.

normalize.py::if hit is None:::#1.note: Same-line dogleg obstacle test: the obstacle-found arm (->1213) is reachable only via the same-line regime whose down-move crowds the next-row header (#698); the no-obstacle arm (->1211) only fires when the dogleg pass runs on a topology with exempt obstacles. Both await a clean dogleg fixture (#698 / #702).

normalize.py::if grp[0].dips_down != t.d.note: Opposite-dip grouping arm (->450): a channel group holding both an up-dipping and a down-dipping bypass trunk. Reached this slice only incidentally via the now-withdrawn dogleg fixtures (#702); not itself an eager-bundling violation. Exercise with a dedicated clean opposite-dip fixture.

normalize.py::if id(t.route) in skip:::#.note: _dogleg_off_exempt_trunks first-loop skip of an already-bundled route (->1199), reached only when the dogleg pass runs; the topologies that exercised it are the same-line (#698) and distinct-line eager-bundling (#702) doglegs. Exercise with a clean fixture once those render cleanly.

Distinct-line dogleg second-loop skip of an already-bundled route (->1236), reached only when the distinct dogleg runs - the eager-bundling case in #702. Exercise with a clean fixture once #702 renders cleanly.

normalize.py::if hit is None:::#2.note: Distinct-line dogleg obstacle test (->1248 no-obstacle, ->1249 obstacle), reached only when the distinct dogleg runs - the eager-bundling case in #702. Exercise with a clean fixture once #702 renders cleanly.

normalize.py::if any(t.x_lo < o.x_hi and.note: Disjoint same-line trunk slotting (->1278): two disjoint trunks of one line keep separate slots instead of riding one shared trunk and peeling off at the turn column - the early-unbundling violation in #702. Exercise with a clean fixture once #702 defers the split.
